description: Evaluates the AEM 6.5 LTS/AMS dispatcher config-authoring skill's ability to create, modify, and validate dispatcher configurations

evals.name: create-basic-filter-rule
evals.prompt: I need to add a filter rule to block direct access to /content/dam except for images and PDFs. Can you help me create the appropriate filter configuration for AEM 6.5 on AMS?
evals.expected_output: A dispatcher filter configuration that denies /content/dam but allows /content/dam/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,pdf}
assertions: Output includes a valid dispatcher filter configuration section
Configuration includes a deny rule for /content/dam
Configuration includes allow rules for image and PDF extensions
Filter rules follow AEM 6.5 LTS/AMS best practices
No security vulnerabilities in the filter rules

evals.name: ams-vhost-pattern
evals.prompt: Create a vhost configuration for www.example.com that follows AEM 6.5 AMS dispatcher patterns. Make sure it works with the AMS load balancer.
evals.expected_output: VirtualHost configuration with AMS-specific patterns and load balancer compatibility
assertions: Does NOT include Cloud Service-only patterns
Configuration handles AMS load balancer headers properly
Follows AMS mutable config pattern (allows runtime modifications)

evals.name: cache-invalidation-ams
evals.prompt: Our cache isn't invalidating properly when we publish pages in AEM 6.5 on AMS. The statfileslevel is set to 0. Can you explain what's wrong and suggest a fix?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of statfileslevel behavior and AMS-specific troubleshooting
assertions: Explanation mentions that statfileslevel 0 only invalidates exact matches
Recommendation suggests increasing statfileslevel to 2 or higher
Provides a concrete configuration example with /statfileslevel setting
May suggest JMX or Felix Console for debugging (6.5-specific)
Does NOT suggest Cloud Service-only tools
